title: gmelina ledermannii
intro: Gmelina ledermannii, also known as the African teak or Gmelina, is a deciduous tree species native to Africa and parts of Asia, belonging to the family Verbenaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. Gmelina ledermannii has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, offering benefits like pain relief, wound healing, and immune system support. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in some parts of the world as a source of timber.
